items.caption: A map illustrating the rise of the byzantine empire, ad 306-424. it shows the territorial changes and military campaigns of the roman empire during this period. the map is color-coded to represent different regions and campaigns, with various colors indicating the extent of roman control. the map includes labels for cities and regions, such as constantinople, gaul, britain, and africa, among others. the map also includes a legend explaining the color coding and symbols used to represent different types of information, such as roman frontiers, campaigns, and battles. the map is detailed, showing the strategic locations of military campaigns and the shifting borders of the empire.
